message:
perf(obligation): add lazy iterators and budget-bound scanning for conflict/violation recovery

The recovery governor previously materialized all conflicts and all linearity
violations into Vec before processing them, even though the recovery budget
might only consume a handful. On ledgers with thousands of obligations this
caused O(N) allocations that were immediately discarded.

Changes:
- CrdtObligationLedger: new conflicts_iter() and linearity_violations_iter()
  methods that return lazy iterators; the existing Vec-returning methods now
  delegate to these + .collect() for backward compatibility
- RecoveryGovernor::run_recovery(): conflicts and linearity violations are
  now collected with .take(budget) so only the needed subset is materialized
- find_stale() now accepts a limit parameter and applies .take(limit) to
  bound the stale-obligation scan as well
- All three recovery sub-phases (stale, conflict, linearity) now respect
  the remaining budget from the start of iteration, not just in the
  processing loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
